rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Use component type annexes for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Use implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain scope consistency between AADL architecture and AGREE annex blocks
Avoid using AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
Ensure type consistency between helper declarations and their expressions in AGREE annexes
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in component type annexes when concrete bounds are available
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Avoid encoding AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
